channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Harris and Biden head to the Southeast for firsthand looks at damage from Helene
item.description: GREENVILLE, S.C. (AP) — Over the past four years, President Joe Biden has jetted off to survey damage and console victims after tornadoes, wildfires and tropical storms. It's not a role that Kamala Harris has played as vice president.
item.dc:creator: CHRIS MEGERIAN and COLLEEN LONG, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: LAPD: Parents turn in their kids for flash mob robberies at 7-Eleven stores
item.description: Parents of at least three teens caught on video have turned them into LAPD for their alleged roles in Los Angeles-area convenience store flash mob robberies.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: California Gov. Newsom signs bills to protect children from AI deepfake nudes
item.description: California Gov. Gavin Newsom signed legislation on Sunday to help protect minors from harmful sexual imagery of children created through the misuse of artificial intelligence tools.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Tribes celebrate the end of the largest dam removal project in US history
item.description: The largest dam removal project in U.S. history was completed Wednesday, marking a major victory for tribes in the region who fought for decades to free hundreds of miles of the Klamath River near the California-Oregon border.
item.dc:creator: HALLIE GOLDEN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Javier Bardem on Gaza: ‘We cannot remain indifferent’ in call for hostage release and cease-fire
item.description: Javier Bardem was no longer comfortable being silent on Gaza. The Spanish actor spoke out about the Israeli-Hamas conflict upon accepting an award at the San Sebastian Film Festival last week.
item.dc:creator: LINDSEY BAHR, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Chevy and GMC truck owners could get up to $12,700 as part of $35M settlement
item.description: Owners of the affected trucks could get anywhere from $400 to $12,712. Here's how to tell if you qualify.
item.dc:creator: Mac Bell, KRON4

item.title: Online voting in Alaska's Fat Bear Week contest starts after an attack killed 1 contestant
item.description: ANCHORAGE, Alaska (AP) — Let the chunk-off begin. Voting starts Wednesday in the annual Fat Bear Week contest at Alaska’s Katmai National Park and Preserve, with viewers picking their favorite among a dozen brown bears fattened up to survive the winter.
item.dc:creator: MARK THIESSEN, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Harris backs striking port workers, knocks Trump
item.description: Vice President Kamala Harris voiced her support Wednesday for striking port workers while bashing her rival former President Donald Trump after tens of thousands of longshoremen at ports along the East Coast and Gulf of Mexico walked off the job.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Helene's flooding swept away 11 workers at a Tennessee factory. Now the state is investigating
item.description: NASHVILLE, Tenn. (AP) — Tennessee state authorities said Wednesday they are investigating the company that owns a plastics factory where 11 workers were swept away by cataclysmic flooding unleashed by Hurricane Helene.
item.dc:creator: JONATHAN MATTISE and CEDAR ATTANASIO, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Hospitals mostly rebound after Helene knocked out power and flooded areas
item.description: Hospitals and health care organizations in the Southeast largely stayed open and functioning during Hurricane Helene to provide essential care for their communities.
item.dc:creator: Devna Bose, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Port strike may hike prices, cause shortages of certain goods: What to know
item.description: U.S. ports from Maine to Texas shut down Tuesday when the union representing about 45,000 dockworkers went on strike for the first time since 1977.
item.dc:creator: Mae Anderson, WGN 9

item.title: Parents spot kids in 7-Eleven robbery footage, turn them in to police
item.description: In each instance, the crew rolled up to the stores on bicycles during the evening hours and stole items, damaged property and left without paying, police said.
item.dc:creator: Josh DuBose, Fox 5

item.title: Tile's Back With Trackers That One-Up AirTags
item.description: Remember Tile? They were one of the first Bluetooth tracking tags, and now they have four new products. The company—fresh off its Life360 acquisition—hopes to stand out in a world of AirTags and Android Trackers with unique features.
item.dc:creator: Rich DeMuro, KTLA 5

item.title: Man sentenced to 6 months in prison for effort to create giant sheep hybrids
item.description: A Montana man was sentenced to six months in prison for crimes committed during his effort to create a giant sheep hybrid for captive hunting, the Department of Justice (DOJ) said Monday.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Memorable moments from the vice presidential debate
item.description: Vice presidential nominees Sen. JD Vance (R-Ohio) and Minnesota Gov. Tim Walz (D) clashed Tuesday in what is likely to be the last debate of this presidential election season.
item.dc:creator: Sarakshi Rai, ABC 4

item.title: Pennsylvania Republican in key swing-state Senate race backs using military to fight fentanyl
item.description: STEELTON, Pa. (AP) — The Republican challenger trying to flip the U.S. Senate seat in swing-state Pennsylvania said he’ll press for U.S.
item.dc:creator: MARC LEVY, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Water and power outages test patience in the Carolinas and Georgia
item.description: Cadaver dogs and search crews trudged through knee-deep muck and debris in the mountains of western North Carolina looking for more victims.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: 11 Tennessee factory workers swept away in Helene floodwaters, company responds to evacuation decision
item.description: Workers from a Tennessee factory say they should not have been at work the day Hurricane Helene caused flash flooding in the area, as some did not make it home.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: WWII destroyer found off California coast 76 years later
item.description: A surviving ship from WWII that was used by both Allied and Axis forces in the Pacific and that was given a ceremonial burial at sea has finally been located after more than seven decades underwater.
item.dc:creator: Matthew Nobert, ABC 4

item.title: Walz misleadingly claims to have been in Hong Kong during period tied to Tiananmen Square massacre
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Multiple news reports indicate that Democratic vice presidential nominee Minnesota Gov.
item.dc:creator: JOSH BOAK and DIDI TANG Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Trump downplays troop injuries in 2020 missile strike: 'You mean because they had a headache?'
item.description: "So first of all, injured. What does injured mean? Injured means — you mean because they had a headache? Because the bombs never hit the fort," Trump said.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Cook Political Report shifts Texas Senate race toward Democrats
item.description: The Cook Political Report on Tuesday shifted Texas’s Senate race from "likely Republican" to "lean Republican," signaling momentum for Rep. Colin Allred's (D) challenge against incumbent Sen. Ted Cruz (R) in the red state.
item.dc:creator: Julia Manchester, NewsNation

item.title: The Latest: Helene's death toll rises to more than 130 as supplies are rushed to areas in need
item.description: A crisis unfolded in Asheville, North Carolina, as officials pledged to get more water, food and other supplies to flood-stricken areas without power and cellular service Monday, days after Hurricane Helene ripped across the U.S. Southeast. The death toll from the storm surpassed 130.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: NYC Mayor Eric Adams accepted harmless 'courtesies,' not bribes, his lawyer says
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — New York City Mayor Eric Adams launched a legal attack on the federal corruption case against him Monday, with his attorney asking a judge to toss out bribery charges and then holding a combative news conference accusing prosecutors of ethical lapses.
item.dc:creator: JAKE OFFENHARTZ, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Donate today to help Red Cross efforts across 10 states
item.description: Days after Hurricane Helene's deadly force tore a path north from the Gulf Coast, many Americans remained without power, water and other basic necessities.
item.dc:creator: Bill Disbrow, Fox 5

item.title: Trump suspect pleads not guilty in attempted assassination
item.description: The man accused of attempting to assassinate former President Trump at one of his Florida golf courses earlier this month has pleaded not guilty to five federal charges, according to The Associated Press.
item.dc:creator: Ella Lee, WGN 9

item.title: Donald Trump suggests 'one rough hour' of policing will end theft
item.description: COLUMBIA, S.C. (AP) — Donald Trump has suggested that “one rough hour” of law enforcement action would tamp down retail theft, an echo of his longstanding support for more aggressive and potentially violent policing.
item.dc:creator: MEG KINNARD, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Basketball Hall of Famer Dikembe Mutombo dies at 58
item.description: Mutombo starred in the NBA for 18 seasons and is considered to be one of the greatest defensive players in the history of the sport.
item.dc:creator: Brayden Stamps, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Kris Kristofferson, singer-songwriter and actor, dies at 88
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — Kris Kristofferson, a Rhodes scholar with a deft writing style and rough charisma who became a country music superstar and an A-list Hollywood actor, has died. Kristofferson died at his home on Maui, Hawaii, on Saturday, family spokeswoman Ebie McFarland said in an email.
item.dc:creator: ANDREW DALTON and KRISTIN M. HALL, Associated Press, KTLA 5

item.title: California leads the nation in this lucrative industry
item.description: It's been more than a century since the legendary gold rush of the mid-1800s, and the precious metal is still found in parts of the U.S. today.
item.dc:creator: Spencer Kristensen, KTLA 5

item.title: Lawmakers fear 'chaos' if Trump-Harris race goes to the wire
item.description: Lawmakers in both parties are bracing themselves for a messy aftermath to Election Day as polls show the race between former President Trump and Vice President Harris is so close in several battleground states that it could take days to determine the winner.
item.dc:creator: Alexander Bolton, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Stuck NASA astronauts welcome SpaceX capsule that'll bring them home next year
item.description: CAPE CANAVERAL, Fla. (AP) — The two astronauts stuck at the International Space Station since June welcomed their new ride home with Sunday’s arrival of a SpaceX capsule.
item.dc:creator: MARCIA DUNN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Man killed, sister pistol-whipped while delivering pizza in Illinois
item.description: An Illinois family is pleading for answers after a young man was killed and his sister pistol whipped while out together on a pizza delivery.
item.dc:creator: Ethan Illers, Fox 5

item.title: US retailers brace for potential pain from a longshoremen's strike
item.description: With a dockworkers' strike threatening to close ports on the East and Gulf coasts beginning this week, Chris Butler is growing worried.
item.dc:creator: TOM KRISHER, Associated Press, ABC 4
